dialogue.definition: A conversation or exchange between people.
dialogue.example: Good dialogue requires both talking and listening.

breathing.definition: The act of taking air in and out of the lungs.
breathing.example: Deep breathing can help you relax.

automatic.definition: Happening by itself without thinking about it.

controlled.example: She used controlled breathing to stay calm.

voluntarily.definition: Done by choice, not by force.

regulate.definition: To control or adjust something so it works properly.
regulate.example: Babies learn to regulate their emotions over time.

tune.definition: To adjust to match something; to pay attention to.
tune.example: The baby will tune in to the parent's breathing.

synchronization.definition: The act of making things happen at the same time or in the same way.
synchronization.example: The synchronization of their breathing was calming.

synched.definition: Matched or made to happen at the same time.

rewarding.definition: Giving a feeling of satisfaction or happiness.
rewarding.example: Being close to your baby is rewarding.

moving.example: It was a moving experience to hold the baby.

nursery.definition: Related to young children; a nursery rhyme is a simple song for children.
nursery.example: Nursery rhymes are fun for young children.

rhymes.definition: Short songs or poems, often for children.

exercise.definition: An activity done for practice or improvement.
exercise.example: Try this breathing exercise to relax.

partner.definition: A person you do something with together.
partner.example: Face your partner and try the exercise.

patterns.definition: Regular ways that something happens or is done.
patterns.example: Babies develop patterns of sleeping and eating.

relax.definition: To become less tense or worried.

reaction.definition: Something you do or feel because of something else.
reaction.example: Her reaction to the surprise was joy.

notice.definition: To see or become aware of something.
notice.example: Did you notice the baby smiling?

contact.definition: Physical touch or closeness between people.
contact.example: Skin-to-skin contact is good for newborns.
